title: convolvulus althaeoides
intro: Convolvulus althaeoides, also known as the creeping jenny or false bindweed, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are derived from its roots and leaves. Convolvulus althaeoides is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Convolvulus althaeoides include teas and infusions made from its roots and leaves, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
